storyTitle: Display Product List on Landing Page
storyAcceptanceCriteria: AC1: The landing page must display a list of available products
AC2: Each product in the list must show at minimum a product name
AC3: The product list must be visible without requiring login or additional navigation
AC4: The landing page must load and render the product list within an acceptable time
AC5: If no products are available, the page must display an appropriate empty state message
AC6: The product list must be accessible and readable on different screen sizes
AC7: Each product entry should display relevant product information (e.g., name, image, price)

testCases.title: Verify product list is displayed on landing page for a guest user
preconditions: At least one product exists in the database/backend
User is not logged in (guest state)
steps: Open a supported web browser
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Observe the main content area of the landing page
Verify that a list or grid of products is present on the page
testCases.expectedResult: The landing page displays a visible list or grid of products without requiring any login or additional navigation steps
testCases.automationReason: Stable UI element presence check; easily verifiable with Playwright by asserting product list container is visible and contains at least one item

testCases.title: Verify each product displays a product name in the list
preconditions: Multiple products exist in the database/backend with valid names
User navigates to the landing page
steps: Open a supported web browser
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Inspect each product card or list item in the product list
Verify that every product entry displays a non-empty product name
Confirm that product names are readable and not truncated unexpectedly
testCases.expectedResult: Every product displayed in the list contains a visible, non-empty product name that is legible to the user
testCases.automationReason: Text content assertions per product card can be reliably done in Playwright by iterating over product list items and checking innerText

testCases.title: Verify each product displays an image on the landing page
preconditions: Products in the backend have associated images
User navigates to the landing page
steps: Open a supported web browser
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Inspect each product card or list item
Verify that each product displays an image element
Confirm that the image is loaded and not broken (no broken image icon)
testCases.expectedResult: Every product in the list displays a loaded, non-broken product image
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can evaluate image naturalWidth > 0 and src attribute presence to confirm images are loaded correctly

testCases.title: Verify each product displays a price on the landing page
preconditions: Products in the backend have associated prices
User navigates to the landing page
steps: Open a supported web browser
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Inspect each product card or list item
Verify that each product entry displays a price value
Confirm that the price format is correct (e.g., currency symbol, decimal format)
testCases.expectedResult: Every product in the list displays a correctly formatted price value
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can use regex or text assertions to verify price format per product card

testCases.title: Verify landing page loads product list within acceptable response time
steps: Open browser developer tools and navigate to the Network tab
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Start a performance timer or note the navigation start time
Wait for the product list to fully render on the page
Record the total page load time
Verify the product list is visible within 3 seconds of navigation
testCases.expectedResult: The product list is rendered and visible on the landing page within 3 seconds of page load under normal network conditions
testCases.automationReason: Playwright supports performance timing APIs and can measure time-to-visible for specific elements

testCases.title: Verify empty state message is shown when no products are available
preconditions: All products have been removed from the database or product catalog is empty
User navigates to the landing page
steps: Ensure the product catalog/database is empty (coordinate with backend team or use test environment)
Open a supported web browser
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Observe the main content area where the product list would normally appear
Verify that a meaningful empty state message is displayed
testCases.expectedResult: The landing page displays an appropriate empty state message (e.g., 'No products available' or 'Coming soon') instead of a blank area or error when no products exist
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can assert visibility of empty state element when mock API returns an empty product array

testCases.title: Verify product list is displayed correctly on desktop screen size
steps: Open a supported browser and set the window size to 1920x1080
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Verify that the product list is displayed in the main content area
Confirm that products are arranged in a proper grid or list layout
Ensure no products are cut off or overlapping
Verify that product names, images, and prices are clearly visible
testCases.expectedResult: The product list renders correctly on a 1920x1080 desktop viewport with proper layout, no overlapping elements, and all product information clearly visible
testCases.automationReason: Playwright supports viewport configuration and visual assertions for layout verification at specific resolutions

testCases.title: Verify product list is displayed correctly on tablet screen size
preconditions: Browser window or device at tablet resolution (768x1024)
steps: Open a supported browser and set the window size to 768x1024 (or use browser responsive mode)
Navigate to the application landing page URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Verify that the product list is displayed in the main content area
Confirm the layout adapts appropriately to the tablet viewport
Ensure all product information is readable and not overflowing
Scroll through the product list to verify all items are accessible
testCases.expectedResult: The product list renders correctly on a 768x1024 tablet viewport with responsive layout, all product information readable, and no content overflow
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can emulate tablet viewports and run the same product list assertions at different screen sizes
